methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $160M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Quantum Computing (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 7 same-sector peers exist in Quantum Computing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: Riverlane

data.narrative:
Quantum Motion is a Series C quantum computing company that has raised $160M to date. The valuation analysis draws from a comp universe of 11 peers across quantum computing—three public (IonQ, Rigetti Computing, D-Wave Systems) and eight private firms including Quantinuum, Infleqtion, and others.

A stage-progression methodology was applied, using total capital raised as a proxy for current valuation and applying a 1.7x step-up multiplier typical of Series C progression. This approach yielded an implied valuation of $272M. A peer-median triangulation was not feasible; while seven same-sector peers exist, filtering for stage, timing, and valuation tier did not yield the three minimum comparable datapoints required to establish reliable multiples without introducing substantial selection bias.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $231M to $313M, centered on $272M. This estimate rests primarily on stage-based precedent rather than traded or recently funded peer data, introducing material uncertainty around the appropriate step-up multiple. Refinement would require either additional Series C quantum computing data or clarity on Quantum Motion's technical differentiation relative to the identified peer set. The tightness of the range reflects methodological constraints, not high conviction in the midpoint.
